prompt:
You are shown one page from a SEC 10-K filing rendered as an image.
Item "7A. Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk" ENDS on this page; the next section is "Item 8. Financial Statements and Supplementary Data".
First locate the bottom edge of Item 7A's text:
- If the "Item 8" heading is visible on this page, Item 7A ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.
- Otherwise Item 7A runs all the way to the very bottom of the page.
Then transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 7A at that bottom edge — the FINAL lines right before the section ends, NOT lines from the top or middle of the page. Ignore running headers, page numbers, footers.
Output ONLY the transcribed text, no commentary, no quotes, no formatting.
text:
We are told that Item 7A ends on this page, and the next section is Item 8. We must locate the bottom edge of Item 7A’s text.

Looking at the image:

- The last visible heading is “Investment Risk” — which is part of Item 7A.
- Below that, there is a paragraph ending with:  
  “...would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.”
- Then comes the page number “47” centered at the bottom.
- There is no “Item 8” heading visible on this page.

Therefore, per instructions:  
> If the "Item 8" heading is visible on this page, Item 7A ends on the line directly ABOVE that heading.  
> Otherwise Item 7A runs all the way to the very bottom of the page.

Since “Item 8” is NOT visible, Item 7A runs to the very bottom of the page — meaning we take the last 5 lines of text before the page number (ignoring headers/footers).

The last few lines of text (before the page number) are:

Line 1: We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity  
Line 2: method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these  
Line 3: investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments  
Line 4: would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.

Wait — that’s only 4 lines. Let’s count carefully from the bottom up, ignoring the page number.

Actually, looking again at the image text:

The final paragraph under “Investment Risk” is:

> We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.

This paragraph spans 4 lines visually in the image (as rendered). But we need the LAST 5 lines of Item 7A.

Let’s look one paragraph above — the last sentence of the previous paragraph (under Foreign Currency Risk):

> In certain countries, we also enter into immaterial foreign currency forward contracts to hedge the purchase and payment of purchase commitments denominated in non-functional currencies.

That’s one line.

Then the Investment Risk paragraph starts:

Line 1: We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity  
Line 2: method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these  
Line 3: investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments  
Line 4: would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.

So if we go back one more line — the last line of the prior paragraph:

Line 5: denominated in non-functional currencies.

Thus, the last 5 lines of Item 7A (before the page number) are:

denominated in non-functional currencies.  
We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity  
method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these  
investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments  
would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.

But wait — the instruction says: “transcribe VERBATIM the LAST 5 lines of Item 7A at that bottom edge”

In the image, the text is broken as:

...denominated in non-functional currencies.

We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity  
method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these  
investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments  
would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.

So counting from the bottom:

Line 1 (last): would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.  
Line 2: investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments  
Line 3: method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these  
Line 4: We are exposed to investment risk primarily related to changes in the fair value of certain equity investments, including certain immaterial equity  
Line 5: denominated in non-functional currencies.

Yes — that’s 5 lines.

method investments where we have elected the fair value option, measured on a recurring basis. As of January 31, 2026, the fair value of these
investments was $4.5 billion. Refer to Note 7 for details. As of January 31, 2026, a hypothetical 10% change in the stock price of such investments
would have changed the fair value of such investments by approximately $0.4 billion.
